The **Indian Institute of Management, Ahmedabad** (**IIM Ahmedabad** or **IIM-A**) is a premier [business school](/wiki/Business_school "Business school") located in [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad"), Gujarat, India. It is one of the [Indian Institutes of Management](/wiki/Indian_Institutes_of_Management "Indian Institutes of Management") and was accorded the status of an [Institute of National Importance](/wiki/Institute_of_National_Importance "Institute of National Importance") by the Ministry of Human Resources, Government of India in 2017[[4]](#cite_note-4). It is regarded as the best business school[[5]](#cite_note-5) in India and is often referred to as the Harvard Business School[[6]](#cite_note-6) of India.

Established in 1961, the institute offers master's degree programs in management and agri-business management, a fellowship program and a number of executive training programs.[[7]](#cite_note-IIMA-Programmes-7) The institute was founded by [Vikram Sarabhai](/wiki/Vikram_Sarabhai "Vikram Sarabhai") and [Kasturbhai Lalbhai](/wiki/Kasturbhai_Lalbhai "Kasturbhai Lalbhai"), with [Ravi J. Matthai](/wiki/Ravi_J._Matthai "Ravi J. Matthai").[[8]](#cite_note-8) serving as its first director. Other notable contributors included [Kamla Chowdhary](/wiki/Kamla_Chowdhry "Kamla Chowdhry").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)[[10]](#cite_note-news18.com-10)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![Vikram Sarabhai](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6c/Dr_Vikram_Sarabhai_ISRO.webp/120px-Dr_Vikram_Sarabhai_ISRO.webp.png)](/wiki/File:Dr_Vikram_Sarabhai_ISRO.webp)

[Vikram Sarabhai](/wiki/Vikram_Sarabhai "Vikram Sarabhai"), the Honorary Director of IIMA.

IIM Ahmedabad was established on 11 December 1961[[11]](#cite_note-11) with the active support of the [Government of India](/wiki/Government_of_India "Government of India"), the [Government of Gujarat](/wiki/Government_of_Gujarat "Government of Gujarat"), [Harvard Business School](/wiki/Harvard_Business_School "Harvard Business School"), and prominent members of Indian industry. The physicist [Vikram Sarabhai](/wiki/Vikram_Sarabhai "Vikram Sarabhai") and businessman [Kasturbhai Lalbhai](/wiki/Kasturbhai_Lalbhai "Kasturbhai Lalbhai"), both natives of [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad"), played pivotal roles in setting up the institute. [Ravi J. Matthai](/wiki/Ravi_J._Matthai "Ravi J. Matthai") and several other Ahmedabad-based businessmen also played a major role in its creation.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) [Kamla Chowdhary](/wiki/Kamla_Chowdhry "Kamla Chowdhry") was the first faculty of the institute, and the coordinator of programmes between 1962 and 1965.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

The institute offers master's degree programs in management and agri-business management, a fellowship program and a number of executive training programs.[[7]](#cite_note-IIMA-Programmes-7) The institute's founding director was [Ravi J. Matthai](/wiki/Ravi_J._Matthai "Ravi J. Matthai").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9) Other notable founding figures were the Indian physicist [Vikram Sarabhai](/wiki/Vikram_Sarabhai "Vikram Sarabhai"), Indian businessman [Kasturbhai Lalbhai](/wiki/Kasturbhai_Lalbhai "Kasturbhai Lalbhai") and Indian educator [Kamla Chowdhary](/wiki/Kamla_Chowdhry "Kamla Chowdhry").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)[[10]](#cite_note-news18.com-10)

In 2015, the JSW School of Public Policy was set up with financial contribution from [JSW Steel Ltd](/wiki/JSW_Steel_Ltd "JSW Steel Ltd") worth Rs. 50 crore, the school is engaged in research on policy formulation and design, policy choice, and policy impact.[[16]](#cite_note-thehindubusinessline.com-16)

In 2024 it was ranked first among the business schools in India by the [National Institutional Ranking Framework](/wiki/National_Institutional_Ranking_Framework "National Institutional Ranking Framework").[[17]](#cite_note-:7-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## CIIE.CO

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: CIIE.CO")]

[![IIMA Old Campus](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f4/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad%2C_panorama_2006.jpg/250px-Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad%2C_panorama_2006.jpg)](/wiki/File:Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad,_panorama_2006.jpg)

[![IIMA New Campus](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/Iima_new_campus_panorama.jpg/250px-Iima_new_campus_panorama.jpg)](/wiki/File:Iima_new_campus_panorama.jpg)

IIM Ahmedabad Old and New Campus.

[Centre for Innovation, Incubation & Entrepreneurship](/wiki/CIIE.CO "CIIE.CO") (CIIE) is a subsidiary of the school. It was originally set up in 2012 as a research institution, with a grant pool from [Government of Gujarat](/wiki/Government_of_Gujarat "Government of Gujarat"), the Department of Science and Technology (DST) and IIMA.[[20]](#cite_note-Startup_Factory-20)

* [Samuel Paul](/wiki/Samuel_Paul "Samuel Paul"), [Padma Shri](/wiki/Padma_Shri "Padma Shri") awardee, IIMA's second director (1972–78)[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)
* Ravindra Dholakia, member, [Monetary Policy Committee (India)](/wiki/Monetary_Policy_Committee_(India) "Monetary Policy Committee (India)"), 2016-20[[47]](#cite_note-:4-47)[[48]](#cite_note-:5-48)
* Jayanth R Varma, member, [Monetary Policy Committee (India)](/wiki/Monetary_Policy_Committee_(India) "Monetary Policy Committee (India)"), 2020-2024[[49]](#cite_note-:6-49)
* [Vijay Govindarajan](/wiki/Vijay_Govindarajan "Vijay Govindarajan"), a [New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times_Best_Seller_list "The New York Times Best Seller list") & [Wall Street Journal](/wiki/The_Wall_Street_Journal "The Wall Street Journal") best-selling author, Thinkers50 Hall of Fame 2019 & Coxe Distinguished Professor, [Tuck School of Business](/wiki/Tuck_School_of_Business "Tuck School of Business")[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51)
* [Anil Kumar Gupta](/wiki/Anil_Kumar_Gupta "Anil Kumar Gupta") was a professor who was awarded [Padma Shri](/wiki/Padma_Shri "Padma Shri") for his contributions to management education.[[52]](#cite_note-52)[[53]](#cite_note-53)
* Pankaj Chandra, former director, [Indian Institute of Management Bangalore](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Bangalore "Indian Institute of Management Bangalore") and current Vice Chancellor, [Ahmedabad University](/wiki/Ahmedabad_University "Ahmedabad University")[[54]](#cite_note-54)
* G. Raghuram, director, [Indian Institute of Management Bangalore](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Bangalore "Indian Institute of Management Bangalore")[[55]](#cite_note-The_Economic_Times-55)
* [Dheeraj Sharma](/wiki/Dheeraj_Sharma_(professor) "Dheeraj Sharma (professor)"), director, [Indian Institute of Management Rohtak](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Rohtak "Indian Institute of Management Rohtak")
* [Shekhar Chaudhuri](/wiki/Shekhar_Chaudhuri "Shekhar Chaudhuri"), former director, [Indian Institute of Management Calcutta](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Management_Calcutta "Indian Institute of Management Calcutta")
* Abhiman Das, recipient of Prof P.C. Mahalanobis Medal in 2008 for contribution in Quantitative Economics, and Prof C.R. Rao National Award in Statistics for 2012-2013.

* [Jahar Saha](/wiki/Jahar_Saha "Jahar Saha"), Institute Director 1998–2002
* [Rajnish Rai](/wiki/Rajnish_Rai "Rajnish Rai")[[58]](#cite_note-58)

## Endowment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Endowment")]

The **IIM Ahmedabad Endowment Fund** is India's first [endowment](/wiki/Financial_endowment "Financial endowment") fund set up at a management school. The fund was established at IIM Ahmedabad in June 2020 from an initial corpus of ₹100 Crore provided by 10 founding alumni.[[60]](#cite_note-The_Hindu-60) The endowment aims to grow the fund to ₹1,000 Crore over the next five years.[[61]](#cite_note-India_Times-61) The fund was launched by the Chairperson of IIM Ahmedabad [Board of Governors](/wiki/Board_of_Governors "Board of Governors"), [Kumar Mangalam Birla](/wiki/Kumar_Mangalam_Birla "Kumar Mangalam Birla").[[62]](#cite_note-HT-62)

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indian_Institute_of_Management_Ahmedabad&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

*[2 States](/wiki/2_States_(2014_film) "2 States (2014 film)")*, a [Hindi](/wiki/Bollywood "Bollywood") film based on the [eponymous novel](/wiki/2_States:_The_Story_of_My_Marriage "2 States: The Story of My Marriage") by alumnus [Chetan Bhagat](/wiki/Chetan_Bhagat "Chetan Bhagat"), starring [Alia Bhatt](/wiki/Alia_Bhatt "Alia Bhatt") and [Arjun Kapoor](/wiki/Arjun_Kapoor "Arjun Kapoor"), was shot on the campus. Prior to this, the 1974 film *[Parinay](/wiki/Parinay "Parinay")*, starring [Shabana Azmi](/wiki/Shabana_Azmi "Shabana Azmi") and [Romesh Sharma](/wiki/Romesh_Sharma "Romesh Sharma") in lead roles, also had some scenes shot at the Heritage campus.[[63]](#cite_note-63)
